groups.name: Ants
groups.blurb: Social hymenopterans nesting indoors and outdoors. Identification often hinges on petiole, antennal segments, and odor.

groups.name: Cockroaches
groups.blurb: Nocturnal scavengers found near food, moisture, and harborage. Pronotal markings and size separate the common indoor species.

groups.name: Bed Bugs & Kin
groups.blurb: Obligate blood-feeders associated with sleeping areas. Size, color, and harborage location guide field identification.

groups.name: Stored-Product Pests
groups.blurb: Beetles and moths that infest grains, flour, and dry goods. Larval damage is often the first signal.

groups.name: Flies & Gnats
groups.blurb: Drain, fruit, fungus, and house flies share habits but differ sharply in venation, size, and breeding substrate.

groups.name: Spiders
groups.blurb: Most indoor spiders are harmless. Two medically significant species in Indiana require careful, calm identification.

groups.name: Wasps & Bees
groups.blurb: Nest architecture and color pattern carry most of the diagnostic weight when specimens are not in hand.

groups.name: Silverfish & Booklice
groups.blurb: Small, soft-bodied pests of paper, starch, and damp materials. Often the first sign of moisture problems.
